title: Nicotine: Killer Delivery, Unexplored Potential
description: Smoking was catastrophic medicine and brilliant social technology simultaneously. This piece examines what the public health victory over tobacco left behind — the cognitive pharmacology of nicotine, the sociology of the smoke break, and why the industry's corruption of nicotine science may have cost us a legitimate therapeutic compound. The harms are not in dispute. The losses are more complicated than we're allowed to say.
